description:
If Claim.use ="preauthorization" or “claim” with no encounter end date, Claim.diagnosis.type ="principal" or “secondary” and not linked to any Claim.item  then at least one of those SHALL be within the active period of the used ICD10 code: 
Minimum Claim.item.servicedPeriod.start/ Claim.item.servicedDate, 
Maximum Claim.item.servicedPeriod.end/ Claim.item.servicedDate
Or any dates fall between them.
display: Claim/authorization service interval falls completely out of all active periods of the  ICD-10-AM code for principal or/and secondary diagnosis and not linked to any item

description:
If Claim.use ="preauthorization" or “claim” with no encounter end date, Claim.diagnosis.type ="principal" or “secondary” and not linked to any Claim.item  then Minimum Claim.item.servicedPeriod.start/ Claim.item.servicedDate, 
Maximum Claim.item.servicedPeriod.end/ Claim.item.servicedDate
and any dates fall between them SHALL fall within an active period of the used ICD10 code.
display: Claim/authorization service interval doesn’t completely fall in the active period of ICD-10-AM code for principal or/and secondary diagnosis and not linked to any item

 Minimum MedicationRequest.authoredOn
